California hub Launch Guides Partners
PermitPulse content hub Flagship: Permit History + Risk Report

Guides for California permit research that stays practical.

This hub is built for buyers, owners, architects, expediters, and small project teams who need better permit context before they bid, buy, or move a property forward. The focus is simple: route the address correctly, use the portal well, and know when a Permit History + Risk Report is worth ordering.

Request Permit History + Risk Report Browse California jurisdictions

Start with a guide if you need context. Start with the report if the property already matters financially.

Launch guides

Five practical starting points

Each guide is short by design and tied to real California jurisdictions already covered in the launch hub.

Permit history

How to Check Permit History in Los Angeles

Use LADBS records more efficiently, avoid over-reading one screen, and know when to escalate.

Open guide Los Angeles page
Routing

Los Angeles City vs County Permits

A clean city-versus-county routing guide for addresses that can waste time if handled in the wrong lane.

Open guide County page
Portal use

How to Use the Sacramento Permit Portal

Search smarter, read the results faster, and know which portal gaps still require manual review.

Open guide Sacramento page
Due diligence

What to Verify Before Buying in Santa Monica

A local checklist for permit history, visible scope, and records you should request before closing.

Open guide Santa Monica page
ADU research

California ADU Permit Due Diligence Checklist

A concise owner-facing framework for checking permit history before planning, refinancing, or selling around an ADU.

Launch context

PermitPulse California Launch

See what the public launch covers now, who it is built for, and where the flagship report fits.

Open launch page Partner page
Guide clusters

Use the right guide for the question you actually have

Permit history

Start with Los Angeles if you need a city-record workflow and want a clean handoff into a report. Then compare with the Los Angeles jurisdiction page.

City vs county routing

Use the routing guide when the address could be in a county-served pocket or when someone already told you “it should be LADBS” without proof. Pair it with Los Angeles County.

Permit portal use

The Sacramento guide is built for portal-first research: what to search, what to capture, and what not to assume from one record line. It complements the Sacramento coverage page.

Property due diligence

Santa Monica is a good example of where permit history, project timing, and visible work need to line up before a transaction feels clean. Use that guide with the Santa Monica jurisdiction page.

ADU and property research

The California ADU checklist stays general on purpose. It helps owners and teams gather the right record trail before they spend money or make assumptions across multiple cities.

Fastest path

If the property is already live, skip straight to the report.

The guides help you ask sharper questions. The Permit History + Risk Report is for the point where you need one address summarized clearly enough to forward to a client, partner, or internal team.

  • One property, one decision-ready output.
  • Permit timeline, scope summary, and obvious risk flags.
  • Better fit for acquisitions, design intake, owner review, and quote cleanup.
Request report See sample report
